demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage Plan (Aetna) primary; identified discrepancy between Atlantis address and facility face sheet (wrong zip code 19104 vs 19143); DOB 05/12/1945 confirmed.
home_vs_ltc_determination: SW Sarah confirms Home is the goal, but notes significant pushback from the patient's son (POA) who is demanding a Long-Term Care placement due to fear of managing the BKA stump care.
weekly_facility_update: Week 3: Arthur has reached therapy goals for transfers; SW Sarah scheduled a family conference to address the son's refusal to accept home discharge. PN provided program benefits overview to Arthur.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into Arthur's Jitterbug phone; program flyer delivered to son during bedside education session on 11/05.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached son on 11/12. Son confirms home ramp is installed but remains anxious; PN confirmed the 24-hour post-discharge visit window for the MA and HHA nurse.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date 11/13/2023 and 1st visit date 11/14/2023 entered into Atlantis queue.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/14/2023 at 11:00 AM to align with the HHA nurse's morning assessment window.
narrative_summary: Arthur was admitted for amputation rehab with a complex family dynamic where his son initially blocked a home discharge due to caregiving anxiety. The PN utilized the 3-stage lifecycle to audit demographics, provide intensive family education on the transition support model, and coordinate closely with SW Sarah to ensure the home goal remained the priority. Despite a 5-day delay caused by family disagreement, the PN successfully secured a home discharge with a confirmed first visit within 24 hours.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit reveals incorrect zip code and outdated primary insurance contact.
Arthur's son (POA) refuses to sign discharge papers for home, citing inability to assist with hygiene.
HHA intake coordinator out on PTO; confirmation of nurse availability for Day 1 is delayed.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's son expresses fear about the complexity of the BKA dressing changes at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated family on the specific role of the HHA nurse in wound management and the MA's role in the 24-hour visit.

format_2_triples.situation: Son demands Arthur be moved to a permanent LTC bed against Arthur's wishes.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the family conflict to SW Sarah to facilitate a formal clinical family meeting.

format_2_triples.situation: PN notices the facility face sheet has a different phone number for the POA than what is listed in Atlantis.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited the demographics and updated the Atlantis contact tab with the correct mobile number.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: When the son asks if Arthur is truly safe to go home, the PN suggests the son wait for the Social Worker’s final recommendation after the next care team meeting.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role in determining the safety of the discharge plan and avoids making a clinical safety guarantee that is outside the PN scope.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN schedules a 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call with the son to verify that the DME (hospital bed) was delivered and to confirm the exact time for the MA visit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the Stage 3 checklist for final coordination and ensures the home environment is operationally ready for the transition.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN reviews the surgeon's wound care orders and demonstrates to the son how to apply the Alginate dressing to the stump to help him feel more comfortable.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the PN is performing clinical education and managing wound care, which is the sole responsibility of the facility nurses and the HHA.
